text:
Once upon a time in a quiet little town, there lived a woman named Ada whose favorite pastime was collecting autographs from famous people. However, Ada was unusual; she had an extraordinary talent for talking animals into signing autographs.

One sunny afternoon, Ada found herself at the local park overlooking the beautiful lake. Various birds were flitting about, and a curious squirrel paused to see what the odd woman would do now. Ada approached slowly, calling out in a charming voice, "Hello there! I might just have something here that would really enjoy an autograph."

The squirrel, fascinated, scampered over. Ada whispered into her ear, saying, "Come here. Say hello to my friend."

The squirrel nodded eagerly, then scampered off to a nearby bush to retrieve a small feathered friend.

Back they came, both birds sitting contentedly next to Ada. She picked up the feathered buddy and said, "This one's a great singer."
